title: Corcovado

infobox.value: ([Brazilian Portuguese](/wiki/Brazilian_Portuguese_language "Brazilian Portuguese language") pronunciation: [[koʁkoˈvadu]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese"); meaning "[Hunchback](/wiki/Hunchback "Hunchback")")[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a mountain in central [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). It is a 710-metre (2,330-foot) [granite](/wiki/Granite "Granite") peak located in the [Tijuca Forest](/wiki/Tijuca_Forest "Tijuca Forest"), a national park.

["Corcovado"](https://web.archive.org/web/20110706155435/https://michaelis.uol.com.br/moderno/ingles/index.php?lingua=portugues-ingles&palavra=corcovado). *Dicionário Português - Inglês* (in Brazilian Portuguese and English). [Michaelis](/wiki/Michaelis_(dictionary) "Michaelis (dictionary)"). Archived from the original on 6 July 2011. Retrieved 2022-09-02 – via Universo Online.

["Parque Lage to Christ the Redeemer"](https://www.google.com/maps/dir/Parque+Lage+-+R.+Jardim+Bot%C3%A2nico,+414+-+Jardim+Bot%C3%A2nico,+Rio+de+Janeiro+-+RJ,+22461-000,+Brazil/Cristo+Redentor+-+Alto+da+Boa+Vista,+Rio+de+Janeiro+-+State+of+Rio+de+Janeiro,+Brazil/@-22.9546839,-43.2175744,1274m/?hl=en). *[Google Maps](/wiki/Google_Maps "Google Maps")*. Retrieved 2 September 2020.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [22°57′8.7″S 43°12′42″W﻿ / ﻿22.952417°S 43.21167°W﻿ / -22.952417; -43.21167](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Corcovado&params=22_57_8.7_S_43_12_42_W_type:mountain_scale:100000)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mountain in Rio de Janeiro, home to Christ the Redeemer

This article is about the mountain in Rio De Janeiro. For the statue at its summit, see [*Christ the Redeemer* (statue)](/wiki/Christ_the_Redeemer_(statue) "Christ the Redeemer (statue)"). For the volcano in Chile, see [Corcovado Volcano](/wiki/Corcovado_Volcano "Corcovado Volcano"). For the national park in Costa Rica, see [Corcovado National Park](/wiki/Corcovado_National_Park "Corcovado National Park"). For the song of the same name, see [Corcovado (song)](/wiki/Corcovado_(song) "Corcovado (song)").

**Corcovado** ([Brazilian Portuguese](/wiki/Brazilian_Portuguese_language "Brazilian Portuguese language") pronunciation: [[koʁkoˈvadu]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese"); meaning "[Hunchback](/wiki/Hunchback "Hunchback")")[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a mountain in central [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). It is a 710-metre (2,330-foot) [granite](/wiki/Granite "Granite") peak located in the [Tijuca Forest](/wiki/Tijuca_Forest "Tijuca Forest"), a national park.

Corcovado hill lies just west of the city center but is wholly within the city limits and visible from great distances. It is known worldwide for the statue of [Jesus](/wiki/Jesus "Jesus") atop its peak, entitled [*Christ the Redeemer*](/wiki/Christ_the_Redeemer_(statue) "Christ the Redeemer (statue)").

## Access

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corcovado&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Access")]

The peak and statue can be reached via a narrow road, by the 3.8 km (2.4 mi) [Corcovado Rack Railway](/wiki/Corcovado_Rack_Railway "Corcovado Rack Railway"), which was opened in 1884 and refurbished in 1980, or by the walking trail on the south side of the mountain that starts from [Parque Lage](/wiki/Parque_Lage "Parque Lage").[[2]](#cite_note-2) The railway uses three electrically powered trains, with a capacity of 540 passengers per hour. The rail trip takes approximately 20 minutes and departs every 20 minutes. Due to its limited passenger capacity, the wait to board at the entry station can take several hours. The year-round schedule is 8:30 to 18:30.

From the train terminus and road, the observation deck at the foot of the statue is reached by 223 steps, or by [elevators](/wiki/Elevator "Elevator") and [escalators](/wiki/Escalator "Escalator"). Among the most popular year-round tourist attractions in Rio de Janeiro, the Corcovado railway, access roads, and statue platform are commonly crowded.

## Attractions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corcovado&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Attractions")]

Corcovado's most popular attraction is the 38-metre (125 ft) statue depicting Jesus at its peak, entitled *Christ the Redeemer* ([Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"): *Cristo Redentor**),* and the viewing platform at its peak, drawing over 300,000 visitors per year. The statue was constructed from 1922 to 1931. From the peak's platform the panoramic view includes downtown Rio de Janeiro, [Sugarloaf Mountain](/wiki/Sugarloaf_Mountain "Sugarloaf Mountain"), the [Rodrigo de Freitas lagoon](/wiki/Rodrigo_de_Freitas_Lagoon "Rodrigo de Freitas Lagoon"), [Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Copacabana, Rio de Janeiro") and [Ipanema](/wiki/Ipanema "Ipanema") beaches, [Maracanã Stadium](/wiki/Maracan%C3%A3_Stadium "Maracanã Stadium"), and several of Rio de Janeiro's [favelas](/wiki/Favela "Favela"). Cloud cover is common in Rio and the view from the platform is often obscured. Sunny days are recommended for optimal viewing.

Notable past visitors to the mountain peak include [Charles Darwin](/wiki/Charles_Darwin "Charles Darwin"), [Pope Pius XII](/wiki/Pope_Pius_XII "Pope Pius XII"), [Pope John Paul II](/wiki/Pope_John_Paul_II "Pope John Paul II"), [Alberto Santos-Dumont](/wiki/Alberto_Santos-Dumont "Alberto Santos-Dumont"), [Albert Einstein](/wiki/Albert_Einstein "Albert Einstein"), [Diana, Princess of Wales](/wiki/Diana,_Princess_of_Wales "Diana, Princess of Wales"), [General Sherman](/wiki/General_Sherman "General Sherman"), and [Karl Pilkington](/wiki/Karl_Pilkington "Karl Pilkington"). An additional attraction of the mountain is [rock climbing](/wiki/Rock_climbing "Rock climbing"). The south face had 54 climbing routes in 1992. The easiest way starts from Parque Lage.

## Geology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corcovado&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Geology")]

The peak of Corcovado is a big [granite dome](/wiki/Granite_dome "Granite dome"), which describes a generally vertical rocky formation. It is claimed to be the highest such formation in Brazil, the second highest being Pedra Agulha, situated near the town of [Pancas](/wiki/Pancas "Pancas") in [Espírito Santo](/wiki/Esp%C3%ADrito_Santo "Espírito Santo").

## References in Brazilian culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corcovado&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: References in Brazilian culture")]

Corcovado is considered an icon of Brazilian culture. "[Corcovado](/wiki/Corcovado_(song) "Corcovado (song)")" is a 1960 [bossa nova](/wiki/Bossa_nova "Bossa nova") song and [jazz standard](/wiki/Jazz_standard "Jazz standard") by [Antônio Carlos Jobim](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Carlos_Jobim "Antônio Carlos Jobim") whose lyrics draw on images of the hill. Corcovado has also been referenced in other artistic works (e.g. the lyrics of [Ben Harper](/wiki/Ben_Harper "Ben Harper"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) literary works, films, etc.).
